title: Calamagrostis avenoides
taxonomy: Calamagrostis avenoides is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Asia. Calamagrostis avenoides is further classified into the genus Calamagrostis and the subfamily Bambusoideae. This grass belongs to the order Poales and the clade commelinids. The classification of Calamagrostis avenoides also includes the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Calamagrostis avenoides, also known as chalk grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful for treating various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis avenoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Calamagrostis avenoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
